label: Upstash
description: Connect to Upstash serverless Redis or Kafka databases using the REST API or standard Redis protocol.

form.title: Connect Upstash
form.subtitle: Authenticate with your Upstash REST endpoint and token, or a Redis connection string.

methods.label: REST API (URL + Token)
methods.description: Paste the REST URL and REST token from the Upstash Console. No Redis client needed — works over plain HTTPS. Simplest setup for most use cases.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Upstash REST API

Upstash exposes each database over HTTPS with a dedicated token. This is the easiest path — no Redis driver or TLS config required.

### Steps

1. Open the [Upstash Console](https://console.upstash.com/) and sign in.
2. Click the database you want to connect (or create a new one).
3. Scroll to the **REST API** section on the database details page.
4. Copy the **REST URL** — it looks like `https://<name>-<region>.upstash.io`.
5. Copy the **REST Token** shown directly below the URL (click the eye icon to reveal it).
6. Paste both values into the fields below.

### Notes

- The token is scoped to this single database. Rotate it from the same Console page if it's ever exposed.
- REST API supports all standard Redis commands encoded as JSON — no special client library needed.
- For Kafka databases, use the Kafka REST Proxy URL and token shown on the **REST API** tab of your Kafka cluster.

fields.name: rest_url
fields.label: REST URL
fields.description: Found in Upstash Console → select your database → REST API section.

fields.name: rest_token
fields.label: REST Token
fields.description: The read-write token shown beneath the REST URL. Treat it like a password — anyone with it can read and write your database.

methods.label: Redis Connection String
methods.description: Use a standard Redis connection string (rediss://) for compatibility with Redis protocol clients. Copy it directly from the Upstash Console.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Redis Connection String

Upstash supports the standard Redis protocol over TLS. Use this method if you prefer a classic `rediss://` URI or need compatibility with existing Redis tooling.

### Steps

1. Open the [Upstash Console](https://console.upstash.com/) and sign in.
2. Click the database you want to connect.
3. In the **Connect** section, find the **Connection String** tab or look for the `rediss://` URI.
4. Click the copy icon next to the full connection string — it includes your password embedded in the URL.
5. Paste the full string into the **Connection String** field below.

### Notes

- The connection string contains your password in plain text inside the URI. Store it securely and never commit it to source control.
- Always use `rediss://` (with double-s) — Upstash requires TLS; plain `redis://` connections will be rejected.
- If you need separate host, port, and password fields for a specific client library, use the values shown in the Console's **Connect** panel instead.

fields.name: connection_string
fields.label: Connection String
fields.description: Full Redis connection URI from Upstash Console → your database → Connect. Must start with rediss:// (TLS required).
